summary: DeVault demonstrates his email-based code review workflow using aerc, the terminal email client he built for software development. He walks through his custom keybindings and templates that allow him to apply patches, push changes, and reply to contributors with just a few keystrokes. He claims this workflow lets him review 50 patches in 20 minutes versus an hour or more using web-based forges like GitHub or GitLab. The setup works entirely offline and on low-end hardware, reinforcing his argument that email-driven development is superior for power users.
key_points: Custom aerc keybindings reduce patch review to single-keystroke operations: apply patch, push, reply with thanks, and update patch status
The email workflow processes 50 patches in roughly 20 minutes compared to an hour or more for the same volume of merge requests on web forges
The entire workflow functions offline via isync for IMAP and postfix for queued outgoing mail, enabling work on planes and trains
Magic email headers like X-Sourcehut-Patchset-Update integrate patch status tracking directly into the reply workflow
DeVault acknowledges this is a power user workflow and notes SourceHut is building more novice-friendly web tools as alternatives
money_quotes: With hundreds of hours of review experience on GitHub, GitLab, and SourceHut, I can say with confidence the email workflow allows me to work much faster than any of the others.
Working my way through a 50-email patch queue usually takes me about 20 minutes, compared to an hour or more for the same number of merge requests.
I have often worked through my patch queue on an airplane or a train with spotty or non-functional internet access without skipping a beat.
That's why I like the email workflow so much: for power users, no alternative is even close in terms of efficiency.
In my opinion, it doesn't get better than this for serious FOSS maintainers.
key_insight: Email-based code review, when optimized with custom tooling and keybindings, dramatically outperforms web-based forge workflows in speed and flexibility for experienced maintainers.
